title: Fix PWA 'Add to Home Screen' install prompt not working

requirements.summary: Audit and fix the PWA 'Add to Home Screen' install prompt flow, ensuring the beforeinstallprompt event is captured reliably, the manifest meets Chrome installability criteria, and the service worker registers without errors
acceptanceCriteria: On Chrome for Android, the install banner appears after the page loads and the beforeinstallprompt event fires.
Tapping the install banner triggers the native browser install prompt.
After accepting or dismissing the install prompt, the banner is hidden and does not reappear.
The service worker registers without console errors and caches the root URL on install.
The manifest.json includes valid 192×192 and 512×512 icon entries, a start_url, and display: standalone.
Chrome DevTools Application panel shows no installability errors or warnings.
On iOS Safari, a banner with Share > Add to Home Screen instructions is shown instead of the native prompt.

file_operations.description: Update the manifest to include valid icon entries referencing frontend/public/assets/generated/medifind-icon.dim_192x192.png and frontend/public/assets/generated/medifind-icon.dim_512x512.png. Verify that start_url is set correctly (typically '/'), display is 'standalone', name is 'MediFind', and theme_color remains green. Ensure all required PWA installability criteria are met per Chrome standards.

Review and fix the service worker to ensure it registers successfully without console errors. Verify the install event caches the root URL and other critical assets. Confirm the activate event properly purges old caches. Ensure the fetch event uses a network-first strategy with cache fallback for GET navigation requests. Add error handling to prevent activation failures that could block the beforeinstallprompt event.

Update the useInstallPrompt hook to read the deferred prompt from window.__installPrompt (set by the early listener in index.html). Ensure the hook correctly detects iOS vs other browsers, manages dismissal state in localStorage, and exposes install/dismiss callbacks. Verify the hook does not re-register the beforeinstallprompt listener (as it will be captured earlier in index.html).

Verify the InstallPrompt component correctly consumes the useInstallPrompt hook and renders the install banner when a deferred prompt is available. Ensure the iOS flow shows manual Share > Add to Home Screen instructions, and the Android/Chrome flow triggers the native install prompt. Confirm the banner hides after the user accepts or dismisses the prompt, and does not reappear until dismissal expires or the prompt fires again.

requirements.summary: Ensure the beforeinstallprompt event listener is registered as early as possible in index.html before React loads, storing the deferred prompt on window.__installPrompt for the useInstallPrompt hook to consume
acceptanceCriteria: The beforeinstallprompt event is captured even on fast-loading devices before React finishes mounting.
The useInstallPrompt hook correctly reads the deferred prompt from window.__installPrompt on mount.
The install banner renders on first load when the browser fires beforeinstallprompt.

file_operations.description: Add a new inline <script> tag in the <head> section (before the React bundle script) that registers a beforeinstallprompt event listener on the window. Store the deferred prompt event object in window.__installPrompt so the useInstallPrompt hook can access it after React mounts. Ensure this script runs synchronously and does not block page rendering. Verify the existing service worker registration script remains in place and does not interfere with the new listener.
